title: "command not found: podman" on MacOS with oh-my-zsh
body:
After installing on MacOS the `podman` binary is not in $PATH when using zsh with oh-my-zsh.

Adding
```
PATH="/opt/podman/bin:$PATH"
```
to `~/.zprofile` is one way to solve it.

I'm not reporting it as a bug because I'm not sure if it should be considered a bug, but it might be useful to test for it, or document it in some way. I've seen both #15542 and #18676.

The issue is that the installer is setting `/opt/podman/bin` in `/etc/paths.d`, which is correct, but when using `oh-my-zsh` specifically, `/etc/zprofile` is ignored. Which means `path_helper` never runs and the paths in /etc/paths.d are not set. It's a very specific use case.

comments.body:
I'm also using oh-my-zsh on macOS, and it works without any problems. Which version of ZSH are you using? I suspect the issue might be on the ZSH side.

```
zsh --version
zsh 5.9 (arm64-apple-darwin24.0)
```

Converting this issue to a discussion, since this doesn't sound like a bug on podman side.
